alternatives.name: Mechanical Impeller / Turbine Meter (incumbent)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Moving parts subject to magnetite fouling and bearing wear; accuracy degrades from Class 2 to Class 3 within 3–5 years in hard-water networks; mandatory 5–6 year metrological re-verification cycle; pressure drop across impeller; low-flow dead band where rotor stalls [SRC: web_search — Kamstrup, KROHNE]

alternatives.name: Ultrasonic Transit-Time Flow Sensor (growing)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Accuracy degrades with >2% air entrainment; requires pipe-full condition; higher unit cost vs mechanical (1.5–2.5x); signal loss during transient air pockets; requires acoustic coupling verification at commissioning [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic Flow Meter (premium)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 3–5x cost of ultrasonic; requires minimum electrical conductivity (~5 μS/cm); heavier and larger footprint; higher power consumption; typically reserved for large commercial/industrial substations (DN50+) [SRC: VN_steam_and_air_conditioning_supply]

alternatives.name: Coriolis Flow Meter (custody transfer)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 10–20x cost of ultrasonic; heavy (vibrating tubes); large footprint; overkill accuracy for building-level billing; used only in high-value custody transfer points at plant level [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / estimated allocation [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost, zero lifecycle burden; thermal energy allocated by heated floor area or fixed percentage; cross-subsidisation between buildings; non-compliant with EU EED individual metering mandate; no fault detection; no incentive for energy saving
